data.body:
GROUNDING (3 parallel research streams) before Q1 of the brainstorm for epic 6f2d.

(1) CURRENT ENGINE (codebase map). Thin LINEAR DAG executor via graphlib.TopologicalSorter, single
synchronous pass — NO branching, NO dynamic fan-out, NO loops, NO parallelism; a Burr-tripwire test
(tests/unit/workflow/test_executor_tripwire.py) actively prevents these from growing. DSL = YAML with
scripted steps (uses:) + agent steps (prompt: <reviewer_id>), ${{steps.x.outputs.y}} substitution,
JSON-Schema + linter. Prompt library = git-canonical prompts-as-files (.rebar/prompts/<id>.md +
packaged reviewers/*.md), front-matter + variant overlays; Langfuse is read-replica/trace ONLY, never
the prompt-text source. Render = Mermaid, ONE-WAY only (code comment: "no round-trip risk"). Eval seam
= Inspect AI ([eval] extra), NOT promptfoo (promptfoo only mentioned as interop in WS-G). Reviewers:
only review_ticket is reframed as a (one-step) workflow; verify_completion is bespoke Python;
review_code/scan_spec dispatch directly to runners. Burr is the DOCUMENTED escape hatch the a88f design
named for exactly the non-linear/fan-out/parallel/durable-pause triggers.

(2) INTENT RECONSTRUCTION (rebar store). CRITICAL: there is NO design/brainstorm session log for the
workflow engine and NO verbatim user quote for "bidirectional visual editing." The "core user
requirement" framing exists ONLY in (a) the deferral story WS-I (1f3e, "out: bidirectional visual canvas
editor (out of scope)", justified as "no round-trip risk") and (b) the remediation epic 6f2d's OWN
reconstruction. The original epic a88f was titled "git-native, AGENT-EDITABLE workflows" and framed
editing as TEXT editing with the graph EXPLICITLY read-only. So the premise "a core requirement was
silently deferred" is itself partly a reconstruction; no record of the user ever asking for a visual
EDITOR (vs a viewer), and no record of user sign-off on the deferral.

(3) VISUAL-EDITING ECOSYSTEM SURVEY (web/prior-art). No off-the-shelf tool satisfies all rebar
constraints at once (git-native schema-validated TEXT as source of truth + true bidirectional authoring
+ embeddable/no-DB-ownership + scripted+agentic steps + dynamic fan-out + low adoption cost). Three
camps: DB/canvas-owns-the-workflow (n8n, Langflow, Flowise, Dify, Node-RED — bidirectional but the DB
or a diff-noisy JSON is the master, NOT your git text); code-owns-graph/UI-only-views (LangGraph Studio,
Temporal, Prefect, Dagster, Argo — observability, not authoring); canvas-primitives/build-your-own
(React Flow/@xyflow, Svelte/Vue Flow, Rete, JointJS — the explicit non-goal). Only BPMN/bpmn-js is a
mature off-the-shelf BIDIRECTIONAL editor whose fan-out semantics (multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics)
fit — but its XML co-mingles layout x/y (257-line diff for a 1-attr rename), needs a JS front-end, and
carries a permanent bpmn.io watermark. Net: the polished bidirectional editors achieve bidirectionality
PRECISELY by owning the workflow in a DB / diff-noisy format — adopting any of them means abandoning the
git-native-text invariant. The only constraint-preserving paths are (a) keep YAML canonical + build a
thin text<->diagram round-trip ourselves (which IS "building a canvas", the anti-pattern), or (b) BPMN
as an interchange/editing format with real diff+frontend costs.
